steps.title: Use Prospero's condition
steps.content: Prospero says that if Miranda remembers anything from before the island, she may remember how she came there. He links one recovered memory to possible recall of her arrival.

steps.title: Avoid invented motives
steps.content: Choice A calls the memory daydreamed, Choice B invents reluctance, and Choice C invents discontent. Prospero's point is that one recovered pre-island detail suggests more recall is possible. The answer is D.
